metadata.name: Fiery Temper madness — discard to exile, cast for madness cost {R}, deal 3 damage
metadata.description: Demonstrates the Madness keyword (CR 702.35a). p1 casts Faithless Looting for {R}, which draws 2 and then discards 2. Fiery Temper (which has Madness {R}) is the first card discarded. The madness replacement effect fires: instead of going to p1's graveyard, Fiery Temper is exiled (CR 702.35a). The MadnessTrigger is placed on the stack. p1 then casts Fiery Temper from exile for its madness cost {R}, targeting Bladetusk Boar on the battlefield. Fiery Temper deals 3 damage, killing the Boar via SBA 704.5g. Then the MadnessTrigger resolves (card already cast, so auto-decline is skipped). Final state: Fiery Temper in graveyard, Bladetusk Boar destroyed, stack empty.
metadata.generation_notes: CR 702.35a: Madness is two abilities: (1) a static replacement — when the card is discarded it goes to exile instead of graveyard; (2) a triggered ability that fires when exiled this way, offering the owner a chance to cast it for its madness cost. cast_spell_madness harness action added to replay_harness.rs (find_in_exile helper + cast_spell_madness branch). Script validated: 1/1 assertions pass.

script.step_note: p1 casts Faithless Looting for {R}; Fiery Temper is discarded to exile via madness replacement

actions.description: Initial: Faithless Looting and Fiery Temper in p1 hand, Bladetusk Boar on p2 battlefield
actions.note: Starting position: p1 has 2 cards in hand with {R}{R} available. Bladetusk Boar is the damage target.
CR 601.2: p1 casts Faithless Looting ({R} paid from pool). Sorcery goes on the stack.

actions.description: After casting Faithless Looting: spell on stack, Fiery Temper still in hand
actions.note: Faithless Looting moved from hand to stack. Fiery Temper remains in hand.
CR 116.3: Both players pass priority — Faithless Looting resolves.

resolution.note: CR 608.2: Faithless Looting resolves; p1 draws 2 cards (Mountain and Forest from library).

CR 702.35a: p1 discards 2 cards (min-ObjectId order). Fiery Temper is discarded first — madness replacement sends it to exile instead of graveyard. Mountain is discarded second — goes to graveyard normally. After exile, MadnessTrigger is queued and flushed onto the stack.

actions.note: CR 608.2 + CR 702.35a: Faithless Looting resolves. Draws Mountain and Forest into hand. Discards 2 by min ObjectId: Fiery Temper (ObjectId 2, original hand card) goes to exile via madness replacement; Mountain (drawn, new ObjectId) goes to graveyard. MadnessTrigger placed on stack after resolution.

actions.description: After Faithless Looting resolves: MadnessTrigger on stack, p1 drew 1 net card (Forest), Faithless Looting in graveyard
actions.note: CR 702.35a: MadnessTrigger was placed on the stack. Faithless Looting (sorcery) went to p1's graveyard after resolving. p1 drew 2 and discarded 2, leaving 1 card in hand (Forest). Exile contains Fiery Temper (assertion via zones.exile is doc-only; harness does not currently check exile in assert_state).

script.step_note: p1 casts Fiery Temper from exile for madness cost {R}, targeting Bladetusk Boar

actions.note: CR 702.35a/b: p1 casts Fiery Temper from exile by paying the madness cost {R} (alternative cost per CR 601.2b). The engine in casting.rs detects casting_with_madness = true (card is in ZoneId::Exile and has KeywordAbility::Madness). Target: Bladetusk Boar (3/2) controlled by p2. NOTE: cast_spell_madness action type is not yet implemented in the harness — see dispute.

actions.description: After madness cast: Fiery Temper on stack alongside MadnessTrigger
actions.note: Stack has 2 objects: Fiery Temper on top (just cast), MadnessTrigger below. LIFO per CR 405.5.
CR 116.3: Both players pass priority — Fiery Temper (top of stack) resolves first.

resolution.note: CR 120.3: Fiery Temper deals 3 damage to Bladetusk Boar (3/2). 3 damage >= 2 toughness — lethal.

actions.note: CR 608.2: Fiery Temper resolves; deals 3 damage to Bladetusk Boar. Instant goes to p1's graveyard after resolving (madness does not affect the exit zone of a resolved spell).
CR 704.5g: Bladetusk Boar has 3 damage marked and toughness 2 (damage >= toughness). SBA destroys it.
CR 704.3: SBAs checked before priority is granted. Bladetusk Boar destroyed.

actions.description: After Fiery Temper resolves: Bladetusk Boar dead, Fiery Temper in p1 graveyard, MadnessTrigger still on stack
actions.note: Bladetusk Boar destroyed by SBA 704.5g. Fiery Temper in p1 graveyard. MadnessTrigger is the last stack object.
CR 116.3: Both players pass — MadnessTrigger resolves.

resolution.note: CR 702.35a: MadnessTrigger resolves. Engine checks still_in_exile: false (Fiery Temper was already cast and is now in graveyard). Trigger is a no-op — card not moved.

actions.note: CR 702.35a: MadnessTrigger resolves. Because the owner already cast Fiery Temper from exile, the card is no longer in exile. The engine's still_in_exile check returns false, so the auto-decline path (move to graveyard) is skipped.

actions.description: Final: stack empty, Fiery Temper in p1 graveyard, Bladetusk Boar in p2 graveyard, p2 life unchanged at 40
actions.note: Full madness scenario complete. Fiery Temper discarded → exile (CR 702.35a) → cast for {R} (CR 702.35b) → deals 3 damage to Bladetusk Boar → Boar destroyed (CR 704.5g) → MadnessTrigger resolves as no-op. p2 life at 40 (damage hit the creature).
